framework.name: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 Information technology — Artificial intelligence — Guidance on risk management
framework.scope: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 provides guidance for organizations that develop, produce, deploy, or use products, systems, and services that utilize artificial intelligence (AI) to manage AI-related risk. It is intended to help organizations integrate AI risk management into their AI-related activities and functions, and the application can be customized to any organization and context.
framework.applies_to: Organizations of any size in any sector that develop, produce, deploy, or use AI-enabled products, systems, or services.
framework.penalties: No direct legal penalties; ISO/IEC 23894:2023 is a voluntary international guidance standard. Consequences of non-adoption are indirect, such as failed audits, weaker assurance, contractual non-compliance, or increased operational and safety risk.

control.statement: The organization shall integrate AI risk management into AI-related activities and functions as part of its governance and operating model.
The organization shall establish the context for AI risk management, including internal and external factors and the intended purpose of the AI system.
The organization shall identify AI-related risks across the lifecycle of the system, including foreseeable misuse and impacts to people and society.
The organization shall assess AI risks using defined criteria for likelihood, impact, and risk acceptability.
The organization shall treat AI risks using planned controls or other risk treatment actions and assign owners and due dates.
The organization shall monitor AI risks and the effectiveness of treatments throughout the AI system lifecycle.
The organization shall communicate and consult on AI risks with relevant internal and external stakeholders.
The organization shall record and retain documented information about AI risk management activities, decisions, and outcomes.
The organization shall plan and implement risk management throughout the AI system lifecycle, including design, development, deployment, operation, and decommissioning.
The organization shall tailor AI risk management activities to the organization, system, and context, including the type of AI and its intended use.
